plan.failureSummary: The deterministic run failed because the survey page dynamically rendered 'Age Group' (radio buttons) instead of the cached 'Select your occupation' (combobox). This layout shift/dynamic question set made the cached selector lookups fail.
observedIndicators: The current run screenshot shows 'Age Group' radio buttons instead of the cached 'Select your occupation' combobox
The failed tool call was looking for selector: [role='combobox'] or 'Select your occupation' which does not exist on the current page layout
Historical run logs show a highly unstable/alternating pass rate (approx 58%), typical of dynamic UI variants in deterministic mode

remediationSteps.summary: Clear page.ai cache and retry automation
remediationSteps.details: Clear the page.ai deterministic cache and trigger a retry. This forces the autonomous agent to re-analyze the active page structure and dynamically fill out the 'Age Group' form instead of using stale cached locators.

remediationSteps.summary: Disable deterministic mode or update test to expect dynamic forms
remediationSteps.details: If this survey intentionally serves dynamic sets of questions, deterministic mode should be disabled or the test configured to expect non-deterministic runs so page.ai always dynamically adapts without relying on cached selector sequences.

plan.notes: The survey application ('unstable-survey') dynamically alternates its form questions (e.g., displaying 'Set 1' in the current run vs another set in prior successful runs). Because deterministic mode relies on a static cached sequence of element interactions, it is highly prone to failing when different questions are served. Clearing the cache and retrying allows the AI agent to dynamically analyze and fill out the currently visible form fields.
